audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for peace, grace, hope, love poured through Spirit, reconciliation, Adam/Messiah contrast, sin/death/grace as powers.

verses.restored: Therefore, having been made righteous/just from trust, we have peace toward the Divine through our Lord Jesus Messiah.
Through whom also we have access by trust into this grace in which we stand, and we boast in hope of the glory of the Divine.
And not only this, but we also boast in pressures, knowing that pressure works endurance,
and endurance tested character, and tested character hope.
And hope does not shame, because the love of the Divine has been poured out in our hearts through holy Spirit given to us.
For while we were still weak, at the right time Messiah died for the ungodly.
For rarely will someone die for a righteous/just person; though perhaps for the good person someone may even dare to die.
But the Divine shows his own love toward us, because while we were still sinners/misaligned, Messiah died for us.
Much more then, having now been made righteous/just in his blood, we will be saved/healed through him from wrath.
For if while enemies we were reconciled to the Divine through the death of his Son, much more, having been reconciled, we will be saved/healed in his life.
And not only this, but we also boast in the Divine through our Lord Jesus Messiah, through whom we have now received reconciliation.
Because of this, just as through one human sin/misalignment entered into the world, and through sin/misalignment death, and so death passed through into all humans, because all sinned/misaligned—
for until Law, sin/misalignment was in the world, but sin/misalignment is not counted where there is no Law.
Yet death reigned from Adam until Moses, even over those who did not sin/misalign in the likeness of Adam's transgression, who is a pattern of the coming one.
But the gift is not like the trespass. For if by the trespass of the one the many died, much more did the grace of the Divine and the gift in grace of the one human Jesus Messiah abound to the many.
The gift is not through one who sinned/misaligned; for the judgment from one trespass led to condemnation, but the gift from many trespasses led to righteousness/justice.

For if by the trespass of the one, death reigned through the one, much more those receiving the abundance of grace and the gift of righteousness/justice will reign in life through the one, Jesus Messiah.
Therefore then, as through one trespass judgment came to all humans into condemnation, so also through one righteous/just act the gift came to all humans into righteousness/justice of life.
For just as through the disobedience of the one human the many were constituted sinners/misaligned, so also through the obedience of the one the many will be constituted righteous/just.
Law came in alongside so that the trespass might abound; but where sin/misalignment abounded, grace overabounded,
so that just as sin/misalignment reigned in death, so also grace might reign through righteousness/justice into age-long life through Jesus Messiah our Lord.

translation_notes: Sin and death in Romans 5 are treated as reigning powers, not merely isolated bad actions.
The Adam/Messiah contrast should not be flattened into later inherited-guilt formulas; Paul is contrasting two regimes and their effects.
